text:
A social media post from two Aussie athletes in the Paris Olympics village has gone viral on social media after questionable outfits set tongues wagging.

A pair of Matildas stars have gone viral on social media after an Olympics uniform they tried on was hit with mixed reaction.

Goalkeeper Mackenzie Arnold uploaded a video to her TikTok alongside defender Alanna Kennedy on Sunday local time after the Australian uniforms arrived in the French capital for the Paris Olympics.

Australian athletes competing in Paris have access to a number of uniforms worn across several sports, and the two football stars gave fans a surprise look of an outfit they're not used to seeing them in.

Arnold, 30, and Kennedy, 29, awkwardly showed off the green and gold ensemble which included a flowy skirt, with the caption: "POV: Olympic gear arrived."

The video has racked up more than 9.6 million views in under 48 hours, with more than one million likes, as some roasted the uniforms.

"Yes, they are beautiful! They just aren't the first choice for most sports, especially with that length! I was honestly wondering if they're ceremonial only or even warmups? Unconventional, that's all!" one commented.

"The worst part is some countries made such stunning outfits, and then there's us...." another wrote.

"I'm genuinely trying to figure out what sport and the comments are no help," a third said.

"Australia's kits are always so boring," another commenter lamented.

Others expressed their enjoyment at seeing the footballers in a different kit.

"Please wear with your socks, shinnies and boots," one suggested as a hybrid with their football gear.

"The fits go hard! why is this so funny tho I can't," another said.

"Lol the skirt part is cute," a third commented.

TikTokers have been given rare access behind the scenes of the Olympians' village in Paris, with many Aussie athletes posting frequent content for their followers to provide insight of their exciting journey.

Water Polo star Tilly Kearns has been one of the main athletes leading social media coverage from within the Australian camp, posting multiple times a day since arriving in Paris last week.

Olympic gold medallist in the canoe slalom, Jess Fox has also shared popular videos on TikTok showcasing how large the Australian headquarters is in the Olympic village.

One video shows how each country's building in the village is decorated in their own colours, while athletes have access to huge gyms, a mindfulness space, plaza including a bar and big screen, merch store and beauty salon.

The first events in the 2024 Olympics begin on Wednesday, July 24 local time, however the games commence officially with the opening ceremony on July 26 about 8.24pm local time (4.24am Saturday AEST).
